task_completion.description: Create a system boundary labeled 'Patient Portal System'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large rectangular box (system boundary) on the canvas with the text 'Patient Portal System' at the top or inside.

task_completion.description: Add three actors ('Patient', 'Doctor', 'Admin') positioned outside the system boundary
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three stick figure icons or actor nodes labeled 'Patient', 'Doctor', and 'Admin' located outside the main system boundary box.

task_completion.description: Add six use cases inside the system boundary
task_completion.visual_evidence: Six oval shapes inside the system boundary labeled 'Login', 'View Medical Records', 'Schedule Appointment', 'Send Message', 'Manage Users', and 'Generate Report'.

task_completion.description: Connect the 'Patient' and 'Doctor' actors to their respective use cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: Lines connecting the 'Patient' actor to 'Login', 'View Medical Records', 'Schedule Appointment', and 'Send Message'. Lines connecting the 'Doctor' actor to 'Login', 'View Medical Records', 'Send Message', and 'Generate Report'.

task_completion.description: Connect the 'Admin' actor to its respective use cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: Lines connecting the 'Admin' actor to 'Login', 'Manage Users', and 'Generate Report'.

task_completion.description: Save the completed diagram to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application title bar shows 'PatientPortal_UseCaseDiagram.eddx' or a save dialog confirming the file is being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/PatientPortal_UseCaseDiagram.eddx'.

integrity.description: The agent used a diagramming GUI application (like EdrawMax) to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the diagramming application interface with the canvas being actively edited, rather than terminal commands or a text editor generating XML/EDDX files directly.

integrity.description: The diagram uses actual UML shapes and connectors
integrity.visual_evidence: The actors are recognizable UML actor shapes (stick figures), use cases are ovals, and connection lines snap to the elements, typical of diagramming software behavior.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write the requirements in a text document
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual output is a graphical UML Use Case diagram, not a text file or script containing the required labels.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is '/home/ga/Documents/PatientPortal_UseCaseDiagram.eddx'. The diagram must contain the exact labels: 'Patient', 'Doctor', 'Admin', 'Login', 'Medical Records', 'Appointment', 'Message', 'Manage Users', 'Report', and 'Portal System'.
